symptoms.symptom_name: Decades of slowly progressive exertional dyspnoea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A slowly progressive exertional-dyspnoea course over years is the classic natural history of tightening mitral stenosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute rest dyspnoea and orthopnoea (sleeps propped on pillows)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthopnoea and rest dyspnoea indicate raised pulmonary venous pressure and acute decompensation.
Pulmonary venous hypertension from mitral stenosis can rupture dilated bronchial/pulmonary anastomoses, causing haemoptysis.
Haemoptysis with acute dyspnoea and hypoxia mandates excluding PE with imaging — not possible remotely.
A newly irregular, rapid heartbeat is the patient-reportable proxy for new AF — the probable trigger of decompensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unable to complete a full sentence in one breath

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to speak in full sentences marks severe respiratory distress and mandates emergency escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Childhood acute rheumatic fever and recurrent childhood pharyngitis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute rheumatic fever is the direct antecedent cause of chronic rheumatic mitral valve disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reddish-purple malar flushing across both cheeks

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Malar flush (mitral facies) is associated with long-standing severe mitral stenosis; here it is video-observable and so patient-offerable, but non-specific and unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: SpO2 92% on room air (home fingertip oximeter)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypoxia supports significant gas-exchange impairment; patient-obtainable and therefore admissible in telehealth, but unverified and single.
Peripheral oedema is a non-specific sign of fluid overload / right-heart involvement; video-observable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever and of purulent sputum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever and non-purulent sputum reduce the likelihood of an infective cause for the haemoptysis and breathlessness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Loud S1, opening snap and low-pitched mid-diastolic apical rumble

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The loud S1 + opening snap + mid-diastolic rumble triad is the classic auscultatory signature of mitral stenosis. Requires a stethoscope — sealed, clinician-only, not obtainable via telehealth.
Bibasal crackles indicate pulmonary congestion/oedema. Requires auscultation — sealed, clinician-only.

symptoms.symptom_name: Irregularly irregular pulse / 12-lead ECG confirming atrial fibrillation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A 12-lead ECG is the definitive confirmation of AF. A consumer wearable cannot diagnose it reliably — sealed as a clinician investigation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transthoracic echocardiogram: mitral valve calcification, leaflet fusion and stenosis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Echocardiography is the gold-standard confirmation of mitral stenosis severity and valve morphology — a hospital investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute cardiogenic pulmonary congestion / impending pulmonary oedema
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Rest dyspnoea + orthopnoea + haemoptysis + hypoxia together define a life-threatening acute decompensation demanding immediate escalation.

Childhood rheumatic fever, decades of progressive exertional dyspnoea, malar flush and the auscultatory/echo signature identify the underlying valve lesion — but the confirmatory members are clinician/equipment-only.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer for in-hospital assessment (the AI Doctor's action, not an outpatient test order)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The correct telehealth 'investigation' is escalation: continuous monitoring, oxygen and IV therapy are hospital/paramedic-delivered.
Confirms atrial fibrillation and rate — done by paramedics/hospital, not the patient.
Defines mitral valve morphology and stenosis severity and guides valvuloplasty vs replacement — inpatient investigation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Chest X-ray and CT pulmonary angiogram
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assess pulmonary congestion and exclude pulmonary embolism as the cause of acute dyspnoea and haemoptysis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home spirometry / peak flow at this moment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Irrelevant and unsafe to attempt in an acutely breathless, hypoxic patient — it delays escalation and adds exertion.
